question_html:
Identify the parts of speech of the underlined words of the following sentence:

(a) People from all walks of life ended the meeting.

(b) Thou art not so unkind. 

(c) Adolescents are different both from young children and adults.

(d) He is small but mighty. 

(e) The very idea of it is absurd.
solution_html:
(a) noun

(b) verb

(c) adjective

(d) adjective

(e) adjective

Mark Distribution:

(0.5×5) = 2.5 marks for identifying the correct parts of speech of the underlined words.

question_html:
Complete the following sentences:

(a) The teacher brought three ____ (bookcase / bookcases) with him.

(b) The man bought five ____ (dozen / dozens) bananas from the shop.

(c) Time is money and money ____ (save / saves) time.

(d) People may have many ____ (hobby / hobbies) based on their interest.

(e) We are going for a tour to the Sundarbans for five ____ (day / days)
solution_html:
(a) bookcases (b) dozen (c) saves (d) hobbies (e) days

Mark Distribution:

(0.5×5) = 2.5 marks for completing the following sentences.

question_html: Write a short paragraph on “An Ideal University” within 10 sentences.
solution_html:
An Ideal University

An ideal university is a place where knowledge, innovation, and diversity thrive together. It offers a safe, inclusive environment that encourages critical thinking and open dialogue. Professors are not just experts in their fields but also passionate mentors who inspire students. The curriculum balances theory with practical experience, preparing students for real-world challenges. Cutting-edge research facilities and access to the latest technology support academic excellence. Students from different backgrounds feel valued and included, fostering global perspectives. Sustainability and community engagement are core values, guiding both campus operations and educational goals. The university promotes mental well-being and personal growth alongside academic success. Opportunities for internships, study abroad, and interdisciplinary learning are abundant. Ultimately, an ideal university shapes responsible, curious, and compassionate leaders for the future.

Marks Distribution:

For writing the Title "An Ideal University" – 0.5 mark.

For mentioning the key features that define an ideal university – 01 mark.

(Examples may include: Knowledge and innovation, Safe and inclusive environment, Access to technology, Global perspective, Diversity, Qualified and inspiring professors.)

For explaining the importance of an ideal university – 3.5 marks

Look for points such as: (Prepares students for real-world challenges, fosters personal growth and leadership, promotes research and academic excellence, supports mental well-being and critical thinking, Contributes to sustainable and responsible global citizenship)

* Instructions:

*Must be written in one single paragraph. If more than one paragraph: 50% of deserved marks will be deducted.

*Must be within 10 Sentences. If more than 10 Sentences: 0.5 mark will be deducted from deserved total.

* For every 3 grammar or spelling mistakes, deduct 01 mark from the total.
